title: Yosys gives judgemental, unprofessional warning messages
body:
Yosys gives unprofessional warning messages when encountering certain comments.  While these are indicative of poor coding style that is proprietary, a professional tool shouldn't berate the user for their choice of code, and should instead warn the user about nonstandard coding practices.

Additionally, the warning should indicate where the issue was found, noting the filename and line number.

## Expected Behavior

```verilog
// synopsys translate_off
`include "timescale.v"
// synopsys translate_on
```

`yosys read_verilog i2c_master_bit.v`

```
Warning: i2c_master_bit_ctl.v:137: Start of vendor-specific `translate_off` comment section
For portability, it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead
```

## Observed Behavior

```verilog
// synopsys translate_off
`include "timescale.v"
// synopsys translate_on
```

`yosys read_verilog i2c_master_bit.v`

```
Warning: Found one of those horrible `(synopsys|synthesis) translate_off' comments.
Yosys does support them but it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead!
```
code_blocks:
Warning: i2c_master_bit_ctl.v:137: Start of vendor-specific `translate_off` comment section
For portability, it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead

Warning: Found one of those horrible `(synopsys|synthesis) translate_off' comments.
Yosys does support them but it is recommended to use `ifdef constructs instead!
